title: Low-weight AUC-optimized pairwise specialist blended with tree baseline
summary: Since the metric is ROC AUC, a model trained with pairwise AUC/ranking loss can add diversity beyond logloss-trained tabular boosters.
rationale: Since the metric is ROC AUC, a model trained with pairwise AUC/ranking loss can add diversity beyond logloss-trained tabular boosters. Direct AUC-optimization literature exists, and ranking-boosting work shows pairwise objectives can outperform plain classification when the goal is ordering.
implementation_hint: Train a compact specialist on OOF folds using a pairwise objective: LightGBM `lambdarank` with race-lap or stint-window grouping, or a linear / shallow NN pairwise AUC loss over positive-negative pairs sampled within comparable race contexts. Blend its OOF predictions conservatively with the current best GBDT rather than replacing the baseline.
expected_effect: Adds orthogonal ranking signal and can improve leaderboard AUC even if standalone CV is only similar to the baseline.
risk: Pair construction and grouping choice matter a lot; an overly global pairwise setup may waste capacity on trivial negatives.
